notes: FIRST RECORDING — irrlicht_observes is unknown until we see what the daemon emits when claudecode mutates its transcript backwards via Esc+Esc /rewind. Spec asserts what SHOULD happen: a clean ready→working→ready arc on each of three turns, with the third turn starting from a rewound state. Drop the known_failing flag once a recording proves it. Adapter quirk: claudecode binds Esc+Esc as a rewind shortcut; the recipe sleeps long enough between sends for any picker UI to settle. The post-rewind ready phase is the load-bearing assertion — if irrlicht's transcript_tailer treats truncation as session-removed, we'll see transcript_removed/transcript_new on the same UUID and the third turn's session_id won't resolve cleanly.

text: Session appears in ready (pre-session or UUID, whichever arrives first)
First user prompt drives ready → working
First turn completes back to ready
Second user prompt drives ready → working
Second turn completes back to ready
Spec: 'Session row reflects the rewound state with state ready' — after Esc+Esc, the session should stay in ready (the rewind itself is not a turn)
Spec: 'Subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point: ready → working' — third prompt after rewind drives ready → working on the SAME session
Spec: third turn completes back to ready
